Divinity/Celestial Prescence.md.title: Celestial Prescence
Divinity/Celestial Prescence.md.summary: Celestial Presence are astral phenomena tied to Outer God worship, visible in the night sky above devotional centers. The strongest display is above Hallia, Calaria's capital.
keyFacts: Each Outer God has a unique sign appearing in the sky above centers devoted to their worship.
The strength of the Presence scales with the intensity of prayers offered.
Areas with many Gods' influence produce especially spectacular night sky displays.
The sky above Hallia, capital of Calaria, is said to be the most impressive of all.
The Iridescent Host's aurora brightens the sky during the darkest nights above Hallia.
The Host's rainbow hue can outshine even the suns on days of particularly fervent reverence.

Divinity/Divine Raiment.md.title: Divine Raiment
Divinity/Divine Raiment.md.summary: Divine Raiment is a phenomenon where divine blessing overflows a mortal's vessel, typically manifesting as rays of light around the head.
keyFacts: Raiments usually manifest as rays of light surrounding one's head.
Highly blessed individuals can extend the raiment to coat their whole body, e.g. as holy armor.
Aurelia VII can coat her whole body in the Iridescent Host's light as holy armor.
A raiment's color and sheen are derived from the deity that bestowed the blessing.
Followers of the Iridescent Host have pearlescent, shimmering raiments.
Followers of the Judge of Ages have radiant golden raiments.
Color and form allow for individual variation, but deity affiliation is generally identifiable at a glance.

Divinity/Fiends/Flintlock Toboggan.md.title: Flintlock Toboggan
Divinity/Fiends/Flintlock Toboggan.md.summary: Flintlock Toboggan is a small, goat-like fiend residing in the Shackled Prince's demesne, standing 4'6" with brown fur and hourglass pupils.
keyFacts: Stands approximately 4'6" tall
Humanoid body covered in short brown fur
Has hooved feet
Goatlike face with human features
Two curled rams' horns protrude from his forehead
Has hourglass-shaped pupils instead of round
Resides in the Shackled Prince's demesne

Divinity/Fiends/Sigzil Haight.md.title: Sigzil Haight
Divinity/Fiends/Sigzil Haight.md.summary: Sigzil Haight, the Shackled Prince, is a fiendish prince and child of the Judge of Ages whose demesne lies along the Infinite Horizon.
keyFacts: Known by the title 'the Shackled Prince'
Is a child of the Judge of Ages
Holds the rank of fiendish prince
His demesne is located somewhere far out along the Infinite Horizon

Divinity/Godly Interplay.md.title: Godly Interplay
Divinity/Godly Interplay.md.summary: An overview of cooperation and conflict between the Outer Gods' religious organizations, noting alliances are temporary and wars are fought in their names.
keyFacts: The Watcher and the Pulse have compatible teachings; their followers often cooperate.
The Belvedere has a high population of both Watcher and Pulse adherents.
The Heir and the Compelled share views on an inevitable future, agreeing on major details.
The Iridescent Host preaches dominance; interfaith cooperation is seen as a necessary evil by its followers.
The Heir and the Pulse have directly antithetical teachings, causing clashes between devotees.
The Judge's followers frequently insert themselves into others' affairs, earning derision as often as gratitude.
The true goals of the Outer Gods are described as inscrutable.
Wars have been fought between nations on behalf of their Gods and are expected to continue.

Divinity/Hierophant.md.title: Hierophant
Divinity/Hierophant.md.summary: A Hierophant is one who has passed through the firmament into the Godhome and met the Gods. Aurelia was the first, ushering in a new age for Færrin.
keyFacts: Magic came to Færrin when an unknown force pierced the firmament, causing chaos and societal collapse.
Aurelia, a Calarian peasant, was the first to hear an outer god (the Iridescent Host) and widened the crack in the firm…
Aurelia's passage into the Godhome created a second sun and brought blessings from the Outer Gods to Færrin.
Calaria established a new calendar marking Aurelia's passage as the first day, adopted planet-wide.
Soothsayers use Harrow Decks featuring hierophant likenesses for fortune-telling; each card has an image and personalit…
Current known hierophants: Silfane (IX The Lamp, Provost of Belvedere), Farrow Cross (XI Justice, High Arbiter of The S…
Alaster Fayn (XVII The Star), hierophant of The Compelled, was lost on the Night of the Midnight Sun with no replacemen…
The Tower hierophant's identity is unconfirmed, though their existence is known.

Divinity/Inner Gods.md.title: Inner Gods
Divinity/Inner Gods.md.summary: Inner Gods are deities who exist within Færrin's firmament — whether summoned, ascended from mortality, or small enough to pass through from the Astra.
keyFacts: Outer Gods cannot pass through the firmament onto Færrin's surface.
Inner Gods either passed through the firmament or formed whole-cloth on the inside.
Some Inner Gods are sacred emissaries sent by Outer Gods as eyes and ears on the planet.
Some Inner Gods are demigods — mortals elevated beyond their mortal coil by a source of power.
A small handful are full Gods from elsewhere in the Astra that could fit through the firmament.
Aurelia I first paved the way to the Godhome.
Many on Færrin devote themselves to Inner Gods rather than Outer Gods.
Followers of Inner Gods may display light different from the norm in their raiments.

Divinity/Outer Gods/Eternal Pulse.md.title: Eternal Pulse
Divinity/Outer Gods/Eternal Pulse.md.summary: The Eternal Pulse is an Outer God patronizing doctors and necromancers, embodying the tension between preserving life and pursuing undeath.
keyFacts: Outer God; edicts include healing wounds/treating sickness and pursuing undeath
Anathema: sacrificing your life or abandoning the wounded/dying
Divine Raiment is coppery and oxidized; Celestial Presence is red lightning
Favored weapon is the morningstar; Divine Skill is Medicine
Divine Font: harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification
Domains: freedom, healing, nothingness, pain, soul, undeath
Cleric Spells: grim tendrils (1st), false vitality (2nd), mask of terror (7th)
No country openly devotes itself to the Pulse due to stigma around undeath

Divinity/Outer Gods/Heir of the Plague.m.title: Heir of the Plague
Divinity/Outer Gods/Heir of the Plague.m.summary: The Heir of the Plague is an Outer God tied to life, death, and pestilence, with no patron country but worshippers worldwide.
keyFacts: Outer God whose domain covers life, death, and pestilence
Edicts: abide by life's cycles, aid childbirth, destroy undead
Anathema: immortality/undead creation, despoiling earth, killing juvenile creatures
Divine Raiment is deep red and fiery; Celestial Presence is a bleeding moon
Favored weapon is the scythe; Divine Skill is Nature
Domains: air, decay, disorientation, nature, plague, sun
Divine Font: harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification
Cleric Spells: endure (1st), wall of thorns (3rd), blister (5th)

Divinity/Outer Gods/Iridescent Host.md.title: Iridescent Host
Divinity/Outer Gods/Iridescent Host.md.summary: The Iridescent Host is an Outer God and patron deity of the Iridescent Church and Calaria's Thearchy, favoring her people's prosperity and cultural legacy.
keyFacts: Patron deity of the Iridescent Church and the Thearchy of Calaria.
Edicts: establish cultural superiority, create great works.
Anathema: forgo credit for your accomplishments.
Divine Raiment is pearlescent and shimmering; Celestial Presence is an aurora.
Favored weapon is the mace; Divine Skill is Diplomacy.
Divine Font: harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification.
Domains: confidence, cities, indulgence, passion, toil, wealth.
Cleric Spells: soothe (1st), creation (4th), collective transposition (6th).

Divinity/Outer Gods/Judge of Ages.md.title: Judge of Ages
Divinity/Outer Gods/Judge of Ages.md.summary: The Judge of Ages is an Outer God and patron of The Scale, acting as an intermediary between Outer Gods and championing impartial justice and world balance.
keyFacts: Patron deity of The Scale organization.
Teaches followers to act as impartial adjudicators for inter-religious conflicts.
Edicts: perform just executions, study local laws, oppose corrupt or bloodthirsty officials.
Anathema: killing without thought, executing the innocent, mocking the condemned, false incrimination.
Favored weapon is the greataxe; Divine Skill is Society.
Divine Font is harm or heal; can choose holy or unholy sanctification.
Domains: death, duty, might, swarm, truth, vigil.
Cleric Spells: sure strike (1st), paralyze (3rd), mountain resilience (4th).

Divinity/Outer Gods/The Compelled.md.title: The Compelled
Divinity/Outer Gods/The Compelled.md.summary: The Compelled is an Outer God, former patron deity of the Raelion Empire, teaching predestination, meritocracy, and self-perfection.
keyFacts: Patron deity of the Raelion Empire; now worshipped by Raelish refugees.
Teaches predestination: all have an ideal life and must strive to reach it.
Emphasizes meritocratic hierarchies and respect for superiors.
Edicts: achieve your best self, work hard, demand others do the same.
Anathema: disobeying a superior or shirking duties.
Favored weapon is the whip; Divine Skill is Arcana.
Domains: fate, glyph, introspection, luck, magic, perfection.
Divine Raiment is silver and matte; Celestial Presence is a meteor shower.

Divinity/Outer Gods/Watcher With 1,000 E.title: Watcher With 1,000 Eyes
Divinity/Outer Gods/Watcher With 1,000 E.summary: The Watcher With 1,000 Eyes is an Outer God and patron of Belvedere, teaching followers to chronicle existence and advance society toward an inevitable future.
keyFacts: Outer God and patron deity of Belvedere.
Edicts: chronicle existence, uncover hidden mysteries, advance society.
Anathema: destroy sources of knowledge, hinder forward technological progress.
Divine Raiment is pure white and twinkling; Celestial Presence is nebulae.
Favored weapon is the glaive; Divine Skill is Occultism.
Domains: change, cold, creation, darkness, knowledge, star.
Cleric Spells: penumbral shroud (1st), stupefy (2nd), blanket of stars (6th).
Devotees may choose holy or unholy sanctification and harm or heal font.

Divinity/The Narrative/Aut.md.title: Aut
Divinity/The Narrative/Aut.md.summary: Aut is an unsettling humanoid deity-like figure who manipulates the world through proxies, now hindered by the Voidsong. He resides in the Black and White.
keyFacts: Completely white skin with oversized hands and feet, gangly limbs, and a cylindrical torso.
His head is a perfectly smooth sphere with no features except a massive toothy grin.
Prefers to observe the world and nudge it in his desired direction through 'friends'.
The advent of the Voidsong has disrupted his ability to influence the world.
Lives somewhere within the Black and White.

Divinity/Unknown Entities/Green Father.m.title: Green Father
Divinity/Unknown Entities/Green Father.m.summary: The Green Father is a mysterious Inner God residing in the Verdant Expanse, believed to encourage agricultural cultivation of arable lands.
keyFacts: Category: Inner God
Resides within the Verdant Expanse
Encourages agricultural cultivation of arable lands
Edicts: Tend to the land, grow new and strange flora
Anathema: Tear down existing plantlife, disrupt the natural order
Divine Raiment: Green, floral
Celestial Presence: Occasional green flares

Divinity/Unknown Entities/The Zorbon.md.title: The Zorbon
Divinity/Unknown Entities/The Zorbon.md.summary: The Zorbon is a supposed mind-controlling entity with fungal qualities, whose influence is believed to be repelled by tinfoil.
keyFacts: The Zorbon allegedly controls the minds of unsuspecting individuals.
Tinfoil is believed to repel the Zorbon's influence.
Followers fashion tinfoil clothing, such as hats, to avoid his control.
The Zorbon has been described as having fungal qualities.
The Zorbon is believed to be somehow related to Benjamin Grayson, CEO of Amber Call.

Divinity/index.md.title: Divinity
Divinity/index.md.summary: Six Gods bless the world of Færrin but cannot physically enter due to a crack in the Firmament. They make their presence known through followers in Calaria and beyond.
keyFacts: Exactly six Gods are active in Færrin.
The Gods are referred to as 'Outer Gods'.
A crack in the Firmament prevents the Gods from physically entering the world.
The Gods have devout followers among the people of Calaria and beyond.
The Gods make their presence known despite being unable to enter the world.

Geography/Austrene/index.md.title: Austrene
Geography/Austrene/index.md.summary: Austrene is a high-altitude kingdom known for luxury alpine tourism and philosophical observatories studying the slip, effectively ruled by the Provost of Belvedere and resort oligarchs.
keyFacts: Austrene is a mountainous country with glaciers, ski resorts, and geothermic springs attracting wealthy tourists from a…
Austrenites have built palatial lodges and raised prices, cementing a reputation as a playground for the rich and power…
The observatories of Belvedere study the slip where the firmament is thinnest, funded by wealthy patrons.
Researchers devoted to the Watcher With 1,000 Eyes and the Eternal Pulse work in Belvedere's observatories.
Austrene is technically a kingdom with a king, but the royal family functions more as an attraction than a governing po…
Real power is held by the Provost of Belvedere and a handful of oligarchs controlling the resorts and their land.
A Belvedere observatory chat log references an anomalous firmament thinning above outpost C12, flagged as potentially d…
The anomaly was escalated to the Provost and an associated fane after being confirmed across sector s68-v12.

Geography/Black and White/Pharos.md.title: Pharos
Geography/Black and White/Pharos.md.summary: Pharos is an infinitely tall concrete edifice visible anywhere on Færrin, connecting disparate locations across the planet and other planes.
keyFacts: Pharos is an infinitely tall concrete building.
It is visible from anywhere on Færrin.
One entrance is located at 13 Bright Street in Aurideon.
The building is only visible to those who have already been introduced to it.
It connects disparate locations across the planet and on other planes.
Navigation through Pharos follows a ritualistic system called the "Rule of Three".

Geography/Black and White/index.md.title: Black and White
Geography/Black and White/index.md.summary: The Black and White is a separate world connected to Færrin via the Pharos.
keyFacts: The Black and White is a world distinct from Færrin.
It is connected to Færrin through the Pharos.

Geography/Brithwyn/index.md.title: Brithwyn
Geography/Brithwyn/index.md.summary: Brithwyn is an independent nation that broke away from Calaria ~40 years ago, distinguished by strict limits on Orgs and a reinstated nobility system.
keyFacts: Brithwyn declared independence from Calaria after the Calarian Prime Minister was assassinated ~40 years ago.
After a short but bloody conflict, Calaria's council recognized Brithwyn as independent.
Brithwyn limits how deeply Orgs can integrate into the country, unlike Calaria.
Brithwyn reinstated a nobility system to manage territory and public works in place of Orgs.
The Regent Administrator leads Brithwyn, with nobles handling day-to-day governance.
Worship of the Iridescent Host remains the predominant religion, same as in Calaria.
Chorath Engineering produces the 862 Aventine, a Palladium-Alloy armored vehicle sold in Brithwyn.
The Blessed Armaments Package on the Aventine is restricted to RBA and Protectorate use only.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Aurideon/index..title: Aurideon
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Aurideon/index..summary: Aurideon is a sovereign city-state in Hallia, governed exclusively by the Iridescent Church beneath a crystal dome that amplifies the Iridescent Host's aurorae.
keyFacts: Aurideon is the seat of power for the Iridescent Church.
It is one of two sovereign city-states within Hallia.
The city is governed exclusively by the Iridescent Church; Calarian government has little say.
Entry is restricted for non-believers, though exceptions are made for business.
The city is covered by a giant crystal dome.
The dome is structured to amplify the Iridescent Host's Celestial Presence aurorae overhead.
Most convox of the Iridescent Church are headquartered in Aurideon.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/Sin.title: Sin and Tonic
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/Sin.summary: The Sin and Tonic is a dingy bar in Sableclutch, owned by Illmari Vaino and serving as Iconoclasm's current headquarters.
keyFacts: Located in Sableclutch
Owned by Illmari Vaino
Current headquarters of the organization Iconoclasm
Interior is dingy and dark with tobacco incense censors hanging from the ceiling
Main area has booths along the walls and a bar in the center
Left exit leads to a workshop and garage
Right exit leads to a stockroom and armory
Back exit leads to bedrooms and an office

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/The.title: The Crowded Flea
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/The.summary: The Crowded Flea is a riverside pub in Sableclutch, popular with dockworkers and serving as the main hideout of the Roundhat Gang.
keyFacts: Located on the edge of the Fousan River in Sableclutch.
Popular gathering spot for local dockworkers.
Serves as the main hideout of the Roundhat Gang.

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/ind.title: Sableclutch
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/ind.summary: Sableclutch is a poor southern district of Hallia along the Fousan River, dominated by dockworkers and warehouse employees.
keyFacts: Located at the southernmost point of Hallia
Sits on the south bank of the Fousan River
Dominated by dockworkers and warehouse employees
Many goods entering the city begin their journey in Sableclutch
Considered overlooked by the rest of the capital
Power centers of the Orgs managing it are found elsewhere

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Teller's Run/in.title: Teller's Run
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Teller's Run/in.summary: Teller's Run is a neighborhood located at the northernmost point of Hallia, originally planned as part of a larger district.
keyFacts: Located at the northernmost point of Hallia
Teller's Run was originally planned to be part of a larger area

Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Widdershins/ind.title: Widdershins
Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Widdershins/ind.summary: Widdershins is a poor neighborhood in southern Hallia, situated on the north bank of the Fousan River.
keyFacts: Located in the southern part of Hallia
Sits on the north bank of the Fousan River
Considered a poorer neighborhood
Home to many service industry workers who cannot afford rent closer to work

Geography/Calaria/Verdant Expanse.md.title: Verdant Expanse
Geography/Calaria/Verdant Expanse.md.summary: The Verdant Expanse is a vast, ancient forest a thousand miles wide, lying between Calaria and the Tenchwin Sea.
keyFacts: Spans approximately a thousand miles in width.
Located between Calaria and the Tenchwin Sea.
Contains trees reaching a mile in height, blocking out sunlight.
The forest floor exists in near-total darkness.
Home to many strange and dangerous creatures.
Also known as the Green Sea.
Actively resists outside subjugation or conquest.
Modern civilization has largely abandoned efforts to explore its deepest regions.

Geography/Calaria/Wrenford.md.title: Wrenford
Geography/Calaria/Wrenford.md.summary: Wrenford is a small, half-developed city on the edge of the Verdant Expanse, founded by Calarian Orgs for logging that was abandoned after the forest resisted.
keyFacts: Built by multiple Orgs from Calaria to harvest timber from the Verdant Expanse.
The Verdant Expanse 'fought back,' making logging too expensive to continue.
The city never reached its intended potential but did not die out.
Many residents follow the Green Father in addition to the Iridescent Host.

Geography/Calaria/index.md.title: Calaria
Geography/Calaria/index.md.summary: Calaria is the dominant superpower of Færrin, controlling trade, media, and military globally, governed jointly by the Iridescent Church and a rotating corporate council.
keyFacts: Calaria dominates trade, media, music, and military power across the continent of Færrin.
The Calarian economy is powered by the strongest industrial complex in the planet's history.
Most Calarians work as cogs in large private corporations called Orgs.
Calaria's government is jointly run by the Iridescent Church and a rotating council of powerful Orgs.
Each Org nominates ministers to a governing council, which internally elects a Prime Minister.
The Prime Minister and the Iridescent Church's Pontifex jointly pass laws and handle diplomacy.
Calaria expanded rapidly due to divine blessings, the Iridescent Church, and expansion into the Infinite Horizon.
Calarians are known for intense national pride, including military parades and investment in great works.

Geography/Fenrith/index.md.title: Fenrith
Geography/Fenrith/index.md.summary: Fenrith is a coastal nation whose inhabitants live in underwater glass dome-cities, dominating ocean trade due to their mastery of sea currents.
keyFacts: The Termean Veinlands covers 85%+ of Fenrith's territory: blazing hot tundra unsuitable for crops or buildings.
Most Fenrithi live in giant reinforced glass domes built under the ocean surface along the western coast.
Fenrithi ships travel fully submerged, avoiding surface traffic.
Fenrith is a mercantile powerhouse; most large cargo companies are operated by or with the Fenrithi.
Fenrith is ruled by the Fisher-Kings: the four heads of the largest cargo companies.
There is no state religion; The Compelled has the largest following, but all faiths have a foothold.
Veinlands inhabitants include fugitives, sovereign citizens, and homesteaders who use modified automobiles to cross the…
Fenrithi trades include shipping, fishing, marine biology, meteorology, and aquamancy.

Geography/Færrin.md.title: Færrin
Geography/Færrin.md.summary: Færrin is a formerly magic-less planet near the galaxy's core, unique in the Astra for its mundane nature until magic was recently introduced.
keyFacts: Færrin is located near the galaxy's core.
Until recently, Færrin had no magic, no gods, and no stars visible in its sky.
Magic is the one universal thread connecting all worlds across the Astra.
Færrish people lived for hundreds of thousands of years believing they were alone in the universe.
Before magic arrived, the only sentient life on Færrin was human.
After magic was introduced, more than half of humans developed new traits from the magical influx.
Sentient magic beasts also appeared across the planet after magic arrived.

Geography/Istria/index.md.title: Istria
Geography/Istria/index.md.summary: Istria is a cursed, war-scarred land where a roaming corruption called the Stillness threatens anyone who stays still too long, yet people brave it for ancient artifacts and crystallized magic.
keyFacts: The Stillness is a living curse that corrupts anything remaining in one place too long, a remnant of the Dark Ages.
Victims of the Stillness have their skin turn gray, crack, and sprout purple crystals before solidifying like statues.
High curse-density areas cause magic to crystallize into a dense, valuable power source.
Istrians live in giant mobile caravan-cities called striders, constantly moving to survive.
Istrians are renowned for wanderlust; one was the first to circumnavigate the globe.
The Istrian mobile spaceport is the closest any Færrin group has come to leaving the atmosphere.
Istria was part of an old Istrian Empire before the Dark Ages and Aurelia's enlightenment.
Powerful artifacts from the Dark Ages remain untouched across the Istrian landscape.

Geography/Lorandris/index.md.title: Lorandris
Geography/Lorandris/index.md.summary: Lorandris is a loose alliance of coastal enclaves on the Fūlwinter coast, infamous for piracy but indispensable to Edan for its unrivaled shipbuilding.
keyFacts: Lorandris is a loose alliance of extra-continental enclaves settled on the Fūlwinter coast.
Its official economy is trade and bulk transport, but piracy is its true primary economic activity.
Lorandrin pirates use airborne vessels called 'skybreakers' to raid inland settlements.
Lorandrin ships are faster, more durable, higher-capacity, and some can turn invisible.
No other country can match Lorandrin shipbuilding, so all nations buy ships from them.
Despite its power at sea, Lorandris is a small country that could be toppled by most rivals.
Lorandrin citizenship is a felony in other countries, forcing natives to hide their origins abroad.
The sea shanty 'The Black-Tide Bargain' appears in Estañion's Selection of Sea Shanties, p. 56.

Geography/Quiet Below/Nos Mortem.md.title: Nos Mortem
Geography/Quiet Below/Nos Mortem.md.summary: Nos Mortem is a Victorian-style manor located in a fane deep beneath Hallia, owned by Benjamin Grayson and staffed by undead.
keyFacts: Victorian-style manor situated in a fane deep below the city of Hallia.
Owned by Benjamin Grayson.
Staff consists of skeletons, ghosts, and ghouls.

Geography/Quiet Below/index.md.title: Quiet Below
Geography/Quiet Below/index.md.summary: The Quiet Below is a vast underground cave network beneath the continent, largely unexplored and home only to those hiding from society.
keyFacts: A series of caverns stretching miles below the continent's surface.
No known civilizations have ever inhabited the Quiet Below.
Offers no resources that couldn't be gathered closer to the surface.
Rarely visited due to lack of incentive and perceived danger.
Superstition holds that the Gods' blessings are harder to receive underground.
Dangerous monsters are believed to lurk in the depths.
The only known residents are those deliberately avoiding public life.

Geography/Rhædon/index.md.title: Rhædon
Geography/Rhædon/index.md.summary: Rhædon is an isolated nation on the continent of Edan, historically cut off by the Siksithys desert and Tenchwin cape, now emerging as a global trade competitor.
keyFacts: Rhædon is separated from the rest of Edan by the Siksithys desert (land) and the Tenchwin cape (sea).
Before airframes and easy flight, Rhædinan people rarely left their country due to dangerous travel.
Rhædon has been undercutting competitors' prices to break into established continental trade routes.
A major ally of Rhædon was 'bombed out of existence,' setting back their trade ambitions.
Historically, most of the population worked in agrarian fields due to forced self-sufficiency.
Manufacturing has shifted to Rhædon as organizations capitalize on its lower wages.
Young Rhædinans now migrate to the rest of the continent for work.
Asaid Al-Taiiq gave an expansionist inauguration speech invoking Rhædon's rise to continental power.

Geography/Tormeré/index.md.title: Tormeré
Geography/Tormeré/index.md.summary: Tormeré is a strikingly beautiful, democratically governed country in the Iridescent Belt, known for pink sands, paraíba gemstones, and exports of fruit liqueurs and hallucinogens.
keyFacts: Part of the 'Iridescent Belt' alongside Calaria and Brithwyn, where the Iridescent Host's presence is strongest.
National gemstone is the paraíba, a blue dust that sparkles across the ground.
Native cheiro flowers and carrilhão trees (which chime in the wind) naturally perfume and accent the landscape.
Major exports include sweet fruit liqueurs, oils, and a mild hallucinogen called erva-teme.
Most permanent residents are farmers working vast plantations.
Government is fully democratic — a rarity in Edan — but defers to the Iridescent Church on relevant matters.
Calaria acts as Tormeré's protector and staunch ally on the global stage.
Sculptor Paolo Barreto caused controversy with a nude depiction of the Outer Gods, earning censure from the Iridescent …

Geography/Velthara/index.md.title: Velthara
Geography/Velthara/index.md.summary: Velthara is a mountainous, resource-focused nation known for its traditionalist culture and unique vertical strand-cities that span hundreds of worlds along the Horizon.
keyFacts: Velthara's population is predominantly miners or natural-resource workers.
Veltharans were slow to adopt modern tech like electricity, radio, and automobiles.
Cities are built 'vertically' along the Horizon, with small footprints but spanning hundreds of worlds.
Public trolleys travel between worlds within strand-cities, ferrying hundreds of thousands daily.
The capital, Tor's Strand, runs from Star 612 to Void 87 and is still expanding.
Governance shifted from family-led individual worlds to cooperative clan-nations.
Wil's Strand in Star 13 mysteriously disappeared, leaving only empty plains.
The Blain Clan denied responsibility for the disappearance of Wil's Strand.

Geography/index.md.title: Geography
Geography/index.md.summary: Færrin is a world in a tense cold-war era following Calaria's destruction of Raelion 40 years ago, with 9 nations on the continent of Edan.
keyFacts: Færrin is in its longest period without overt conflict since the pre-Aurelia I dark ages.
40 years ago, Calaria destroyed rival nation Raelion on the night of the Midnight Sun.
Under threat of total annihilation, nations have avoided outright war since.
Conflict shifted to psychological warfare, propaganda, espionage, embargoes, and tech competition.
Alliances are described as tenuous and fickle but short-term advantageous.
Nine countries total (including Calaria) participate in this political climate.
The primary continent is Edan, where Calaria resides.
Other continents exist on Færrin but are rarely relevant; their people seldom travel to Edan.

Org/Amber Call/People/Benjamin Grayson.m.title: Benjamin Grayson
Org/Amber Call/People/Benjamin Grayson.m.summary: Benjamin Grayson is the CEO of Amber Call, Calaria's top health insurance provider, and a secret high-level necromancer and follower of the Eternal Pulse.
keyFacts: Human male, ~30 years old in appearance, with stark white hair and gray slate-colored eyes.
Bears a large raiment of the Eternal Pulse.
Current CEO of Amber Call.
Amber Call is the foremost premium health insurance provider in Calaria.
Follower of the Eternal Pulse.
Secretly a high-level necromancer.
Currently resides in Nos Mortem.

Org/Amber Call/People/Mr. Whiskers.md.title: Mr. Whiskers
Org/Amber Call/People/Mr. Whiskers.md.summary: Mr. Whiskers was a black and white mottled catfolk street-dweller of Sableclutch, slain by Benjamin Grayson for a necromantic ritual.
keyFacts: Mr. Whiskers was a black and white mottled catfolk.
He lived on the streets of Sableclutch.
He was a member of the Anti-Zorbon Taskforce.
The Anti-Zorbon Taskforce met in the sewers beneath Widdershins.
He was slain by Benjamin Grayson.
His death was used in a necromantic ritual.

Org/Amber Call/People/Ossain Madrun.md.title: Ossain Madrun
Org/Amber Call/People/Ossain Madrun.md.summary: Ossain Madrun is a skeletal undead majordomo serving Nos Mortem, recognizable by his red glowing eyes and broken jaw.
keyFacts: Ossain is a skeleton standing 5'6" tall.
He has deep red glowing points of light for eyes.
His jaw is broken in three places.
He serves as the majordomo of Nos Mortem.

Org/Amber Call/index.md.title: Amber Call
Org/Amber Call/index.md.summary: Amber Call is a medical rescue and insurance company that provides care to plan holders for any injury, no matter how minor.
keyFacts: Amber Call is a medical rescue and insurance company.
They respond to any injury, however minor, for plan holders.
Plan holders receive both medical care and a bill for services rendered.

Org/Belvedere.md.title: Belvedere
Org/Belvedere.md.summary: Belvedere is an ancient organization founded to chronicle the world, which evolved to include spies, scholars, aristocrats, and arcanists guided by divine entities called Hearts.
keyFacts: Founded soon after the Dark Ages ended, mandated by the Watcher With 1,000 Eyes to chronicle Færrin.
The first Provost established the first chancery in Austrene, atop the planet's highest mountains.
Originally a fact-recording outfit, Belvedere expanded due to pressure from rival Orgs seeking its secrets.
Political operatives ('socialites and spies') were added to learn people and fight proxy wars.
Hearts are slivers of sentient divine energy gifted by the Outer Gods to help process vast amounts of data.
Hearts became coordinators and leaders; many members now report to a Heart instead of a person.
Four member paths exist: academics, archivists, aristocrats, and arcanists.
Arcanists work closely with the Hearts and their divine machinery.

Org/Candescent Legion.md.title: Candescent Legion
Org/Candescent Legion.md.summary: The Candescent Legion is a private army known for being cheap but low-quality, typically used as expendable fodder alongside smaller, better-trained forces.
keyFacts: Is a private army available for hire.
Significantly cheaper than alternative mercenary options.
Quality is considered poor — 'you get what you pay for'.
Primarily used as fodder to supplement smaller, better-trained fighting forces.

Org/Children of the Heir.md.title: Children of the Heir
Org/Children of the Heir.md.summary: The Children of the Heir are worshippers of the Heir of the Plague, acting as her agents to preserve the natural circle of life against disruption.
keyFacts: Serve the Heir of the Plague, who abhors disruption of the natural circle of life.
Consider deforestation, draining marshlands, and cybernetic augmentation as breaks in the circle of life.
Members are called 'Children' and act as the Heir's hands in the world.
Methods range from legal lobbying and lawyering to bombing construction sites and earth magic.
All members tend to adopt an animalistic or floral motif to signal allegiance to the Heir.
The organization's interpretation of the Heir's will varies significantly between individual members.

Org/Chorath Engineering.md.title: Chorath Engineering
Org/Chorath Engineering.md.summary: Chorath Engineering is a Brithwyn-based automobile designer and builder, producing cars for city driving, combat, and Infinite Horizon exploration.
keyFacts: Based in Brithwyn
Designs and builds automobiles
Produces vehicles suited for city driving, shootouts, and exploration
Serves customers venturing into the Infinite Horizon

Org/Concordia.md.title: Concordia
Org/Concordia.md.summary: Concordia is a major manufacturing organization in Calaria that pivoted from magical goods to modern appliances and machinery by integrating magic into their assembly lines.
keyFacts: Concordia operates in Calaria on the continent of Færrin.
They pivoted from scroll production to household appliances like dishwashers, freezers, and air conditioners.
Concordia uses skilled mages to design and operate magically-enhanced assembly lines.
Their assembly lines feature magic automation: self-shaping steel and self-assembling motors.
They manufacture a wide range of goods: watches, televisions, construction machinery, and more.
Concordia capitalized on the rapid technological shift that made older magical goods obsolete.
40 years prior to the current era, magic items like sending stones and light spirits were commonplace.
They are described as one of the organizations that survived and thrived during the tech revolution.

Org/Ghosts of Raelion.md.title: Ghosts of Raelion
Org/Ghosts of Raelion.md.summary: The Ghosts of Raelion are a fractured organization of Raelish survivors united by the destruction of their homeland, split between three competing factions with no formal hierarchy.
keyFacts: Originally a mercenary group from Raelion who were abroad when the Midnight Sun destroyed their homeland.
Founded by Donovan Hale, who swore vengeance for Raelion and all who perished.
Grew from a small merc group into a continent-wide movement of Raelish survivors.
Three internal factions: Hale's hardliners, Bright's religious revivalists, and Marran's separatists.
Juno Bright, a former priest of the Raelic Church, leads a faction focused on reuniting disenfranchised Raelish.
Calder Marran leads a faction that wants to abandon Raelish cultural identity and become an independent people.
No formal hierarchy exists; members operate in independent 'cells' that coordinate only when necessary.
Cells typically align with one faction but mixed allegiances within a single cell are common.

Org/Hildebrandt Corporation.md.title: Hildebrandt Corporation
Org/Hildebrandt Corporation.md.summary: Hildebrandt Corporation is an airframe company pioneering the commercial flight industry, run by a fortune dragon named Hildebrandt.
keyFacts: Hildebrandt Corporation is an airframe manufacturing company.
The company is a pioneer in the commercial flight industry.
The organization is run by a fortune dragon named Hildebrandt.

Org/Hollowpact.md.title: Hollowpact
Org/Hollowpact.md.summary: Hollowpact is a mechanized infantry unit from Istria, specializing in rapid strike operations against enemy encampments.
keyFacts: Mechanized infantry originating from Istria.
Specializes in hit-and-run tactics: destroying encampments before enemies can react.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anaïs Marchal.md.title: Anaïs Marchal
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anaïs Marchal.md.summary: Anaïs Marchal is a reserved, magically-inclined human male and fraternal twin of Anouk, operating within the Iconoclasm organization.
keyFacts: Late 20s human male with red hair and dark brown eyes
Fraternal twin of Anouk Marchal
Wears sharp plain suits with wooden rings and bracelets
Bears a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host
More magically-inclined of the two twins
His workshop section is filled with magical tomes and alchemical reagents
More reserved in personality compared to his sister Anouk

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anouk Marchal.md.title: Anouk Marchal
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Anouk Marchal.md.summary: Anouk Marchal is a human member of the Iconoclasm, a tech-focused workshop tinkerer and fraternal twin of Anaïs Marchal.
keyFacts: Late 20s human female with red hair and dark brown eyes.
Fraternal twin of Anaïs Marchal.
Wears practical workshop jumpsuits to protect against burns and stains.
Possesses a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host.
The more technologically-inclined of the twins.
Her workshop area is filled with multimeters, oscilloscopes, and electrical equipment.
More outgoing than her brother (Anaïs).

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Dura Oil-Drinker.m.title: Dura Oil-Drinker
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Dura Oil-Drinker.m.summary: Dura Oil-Drinker is an orcish mechanic and founding member of Iconoclasm, handling vehicle maintenance, outside contacts, and the group ledger.
keyFacts: Mid-40s orcish woman with dark green skin and curly black hair.
One of her two bottom tusks has been cut off and sanded to a stump.
Wears mechanics overalls and dark welding goggles.
Bears a small raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Serves as Iconoclasm's resident gearhead and maintains the company car.
Handles contacts with outside organizations and manages the group ledger.
Was the first member to join Iconoclasm when Illmari Vaino founded it.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Elias Ramsey.md.title: Elias Ramsey
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Elias Ramsey.md.summary: Elias Ramsey is an elderly dwarven quartermaster for Iconoclasm, known for his curmudgeonly attitude and skill at locating lost items.
keyFacts: Elderly, dark-skinned dwarven male with wispy blonde-gray hair and a short-trimmed mustache and beard.
Wears tweed suits with elbow patches and keeps reading glasses on a chain around his neck.
Bears a small raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Serves as quartermaster for Iconoclasm.
Known as the group curmudgeon — prefers doing things his own way.
Skilled at locating items lost by other Iconoclasm members.

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Gin Soaked Rag.md.title: Gin Soaked Rag
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Gin Soaked Rag.md.summary: Gin Soaked Rag (Ginny) is a sentient dishrag-in-a-suit who serves as the Heart maintaining the Sin and Tonic fane.
keyFacts: Appears as a 6-foot-tall dishrag wearing a three-piece suit
Floating suit arms end in gloves that move independently
His face is formed by the dishrag scrunching up
Liquid beads at his edges when he moves
Holds the role of Heart for the Sin and Tonic fane
Friends with Illmari Vaino and the rest of Iconoclasm
Considered part of the Iconoclasm team despite being unable to leave the bar

Org/Iconoclasm/People/Illmari Vaino.md.title: Illmari Vaino
Org/Iconoclasm/People/Illmari Vaino.md.summary: Illmari Vaino is a scarred half-elf in his late 60s, proprietor of the Sin and Tonic and founder of the mercenary group Iconoclasm.
keyFacts: Late-60s half-elf male with black slicked-back hair and facial scars.
Possesses a raiment of the Iridescent Host.
Current proprietor of the Sin and Tonic.
Founder of the mercenary group Iconoclasm.
Was one of the first to hear the Voidsong.
Gathered like-minded people to investigate the Voidsong's source and meaning.

Org/Iconoclasm/index.md.title: Iconoclasm
Org/Iconoclasm/index.md.summary: Iconoclasm is a mercenary organization founded in Hallia, now the premier group investigating the mysterious Voidsong phenomenon after its members became concentrated listeners.
keyFacts: Originally a small-time freelance mercenary group based in Hallia.
Founded by Ilmari Vaino, who was among the first to hear the Voidsong.
Has the highest concentration of Voidsong listeners on the continent.
Grew as listeners from other Orgs joined seeking answers about the Voidsong.
Other Orgs donate staffing and funding to Iconoclasm as a show of goodwill.
Orgs also fund Iconoclasm to prevent rivals from gaining an advantage.
Player characters are recently joined members, either loaned by an Org or independent.

Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.title: Detective Ellis Brooks
Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.summary: Detective Ellis Brooks is a seasoned middle-aged human detective in the Magical Crimes division, known for his pragmatic instincts and leadership role.
keyFacts: Middle-aged human male with graying hair hidden under a fedora.
Wears an open brown peacoat over a disheveled white button-down shirt.
Carries a pistol in a chest holster worn over his shirt.
Bears a moderately sized raiment of the Iridescent Host.
No-nonsense personality who trusts his gut over dismissing the strange.
Prefers fieldwork but is too effective as a coordinator to justify leaving his desk role.

Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.title: Magical Crimes
Org/Iridescent Church/Convox/Magical Cri.summary: The Magical Crimes Convox is an Iridescent Church department that investigates unexplained supernatural events harming the Host's people.
keyFacts: Department of the Iridescent Church focused on solving weird/unexplained harmful phenomena.
Previously investigated a mysterious curse originating from the Verdant Expanse.
Previously investigated an everlasting rain caused by excessive augury.
Headquartered at 33 Rook's Crossing in the Aurideon.

Org/Iridescent Church/index.md.title: Iridescent Church
Org/Iridescent Church/index.md.summary: The Iridescent Church is Færrin's most powerful organization, founded by Aurelia I to spread the Iridescent Host's teachings, now led by reincarnating Pontifex Aurelia VII.
keyFacts: Founded by the first Hierophant, Aurelia I, as a collective to spread the Iridescent Host's teachings.
The Host granted Aurelia the gift of reincarnation; Aurelia VII is the current Pontifex.
Most influential in the 'Iridescent Belt': Calaria, Brithwyn, and Tormeré.
In Calaria, the Church is part of government and worship of the Host is the official state religion.
In Tormeré, the Church holds an advisory role and the people defer to its leadership.
Many in Brithwyn believe Aurelia VII is not a true reincarnation and await the real Pontifex.
The Church is divided into semi-independent sub-organizations called convox, named after Saunts.
Notable convox include the Suric convox (priest training) and the Forma convox (manages churches of Hallia).

Org/Laity.md.title: Laity
Org/Laity.md.summary: Laity is a military contractor widely understood to be a covert arm of the Iridescent Church, used when deniability is needed.
keyFacts: Laity is technically an independent military contractor.
Laity is commonly known to be another arm of the Iridescent Church.
Laity operates in areas where the Iridescent Church requires deniability.

Org/Opaline Reserve.md.title: Opaline Reserve
Org/Opaline Reserve.md.summary: The Opaline Reserve is a racketeering operation disguised as a brewery in Hallia's Widdershins district.
keyFacts: Operates as a brewery in the Widdershins district of Hallia.
Suspected of conducting illegal augury within city limits.
Under suspicion by the Magical Crimes convox of the Iridescent Church.

Org/Pale Lantern Society.md.title: Pale Lantern Society
Org/Pale Lantern Society.md.summary: The Pale Lantern Society is Færrin's oldest organization, a once-secret cult pursuing immortality through necromancy, now empowered as the worldly presence of the Outer God known as the Pulse.
keyFacts: Possibly the oldest active organization on Færrin, predating the Gods and the Iridescent Church.
Originally a clandestine cabal of warlords and aristocrats practicing necromancy and memetics.
Gained public legitimacy when the Pulse declared them her presence in the world after the firmament cracked.
Their ranks grew with necromancers and healers, whom the Society views as closely related in practice.
Currently operates hospitals, research centers, and graveyards across the continent.
Famous for their Columbariums, where wealthy patrons' preserved corpses are arranged as public art.
Bodies in Columbariums await the Society's perfection of necromantic arts to be resurrected.
Their ultimate goal is perfecting true resurrection to return the dead to life.

Org/Prime Meridian.md.title: Prime Meridian
Org/Prime Meridian.md.summary: Prime Meridian is an infrastructure organization that controls Calaria's roads and rail, and famously executed a bloodless coup to dissolve the monarchy.
keyFacts: Founded by Forster Cain as a small transportation company during the era of horse-pulled trams.
Built the first rail system in Calaria through a deal with the then-king.
Grew to control all roads and rail infrastructure in Calaria.
Jointly announced the dissolution of Calarian royal rule with the Iridescent Church.
The king of Calaria stepped down after losing Church support and control of logistics.
Cain and the Iridescent Church founded the Council that now rules Calaria.
Prime Meridian is the only Org to have performed a coup — and a bloodless one.
Still builds roads and operates trains connecting most of Færrin after Cain's death.

Org/Protectorate.md.title: Protectorate
Org/Protectorate.md.summary: Protectorate is a Brith private mercenary army, historically used to destabilize governments, that now primarily serves as peacekeepers within Brithwyn.
keyFacts: Protectorate is the sole outside organization permitted to operate within Brithwyn.
It is a private army-for-hire originating from Brithwyn.
Before the Brithwyn-Calaria split, it was the premier company for destabilizing governments and waging wars.
While the Calarian army focused on crusades, Protectorate served the highest bidder.
Protectorate soldiers aided Brithwyn in breaking free from Calarian rule.
They now primarily act as peacekeepers within Brithwyn but remain available for hire.
Elite soldiers called 'slayers' can enter a powerful 'battletrance' state.
Stories of entranced slayers singlehandedly destroying rival encampments are considered only slightly exaggerated.

Org/Radiant Arms.md.title: Radiant Arms
Org/Radiant Arms.md.summary: Radiant Arms is a firearm manufacturer based in Calaria, specializing in high-caliber enchanted weapons.
keyFacts: Based in Calaria
Specializes in high-caliber, enchanted firearms
Company motto: "Just a tap'll do ya!"

Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Jasper Glover.m.title: Jasper Glover
Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Jasper Glover.m.summary: Jasper Glover is a low-level henchman of the Roundhat Gang who was spared by the Party and sent back to his gang's hideout.
keyFacts: Low-level henchman in the Roundhat Gang
Was spared by the Party rather than killed or captured
Was allowed to return to the Roundhat Gang's hideout after being spared

Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Tywelwyn Leathe.title: Tywelwyn Leatherhide
Org/Roundhat Gang/People/Tywelwyn Leathe.summary: Tywelwyn Leatherhide is the founder and leader of the Roundhat Gang, a protection racket in Sableclutch, who recently gained magical ability via a deal with Pyrelight.
keyFacts: Tywelwyn is a 6'11" deerfolk with dappled brown fur and tall, branching antlers.
His muscles are unnaturally large, suggesting magical or chemical enhancement.
His skin runs hot enough that his sweat sizzles on contact.
He is the founder and leader of the Roundhat Gang.
The Roundhat Gang operates as a protection racket in Sableclutch.
He was rumored to have gone crazy and to be hearing voices.
He struck a deal with someone in the organization Pyrelight.
Formerly unable to use magic, he now pilots a simulacrum to act on his behalf.

Org/Roundhat Gang/index.md.title: Roundhat Gang
Org/Roundhat Gang/index.md.summary: The Roundhat Gang is a protection racket operating in Sableclutch, run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide out of The Crowded Flea.
keyFacts: Operates as a protection racket in Sableclutch.
Led by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
Based out of a location called The Crowded Flea.
Has recently established some kind of partnership with Pyrelight.

Org/RunGunners.md.title: RunGunners
Org/RunGunners.md.summary: The RunGunners are an armored transport organization based in Fenrith, offering armed convoy services with live scrying and automatic tagging of attackers.
keyFacts: Operate as armored transport convoys for hire, moving people or goods safely.
Offer a live scry of transports during delivery as part of their guarantee.
Automatically attempt to magically tag anyone who engages their convoys.
Based in the Termean Veinlands region of Fenrith.
Their vehicles are custom-built to handle soft, dry sand as well as paved roads.
Their corporate town, Throttlehold, is home to engineers, mechanics, and armed personnel.

Org/Sable and Suns.md.title: Sable and Suns
Org/Sable and Suns.md.summary: Sable and Suns is a military contractor supplying weapons, vehicles, and training consultants to multiple factions across Edan.
keyFacts: S&S is technically unaffiliated with any country in Edan, openly playing all sides.
They supply the Calarian military as one of its major contractors.
Protectorate and the Pirate-Kings of Lorandris both hold contracts with S&S.
Some up-and-coming orgs out of Rhædon also hold contracts with them.
Unlike other contractors, S&S sends consultants to teach clients how to use purchased equipment.
S&S firearms and blades are high quality but expensive.
S&S-made weaponry on mercenaries or bandits signals a wealthy backer.

Org/Sentinel Dynamics.md.title: Sentinel Dynamics
Org/Sentinel Dynamics.md.summary: Sentinel Dynamics is a prestigious security organization offering bodyguard and guard services, considered a status symbol among the wealthy.
keyFacts: Provides personal bodyguard services for VIPs
Offers security for locations such as art galleries
Hiring a Sentinel as a bodyguard is considered a status symbol

Org/Ternion Heavy Industries.md.title: Ternion Heavy Industries
Org/Ternion Heavy Industries.md.summary: Ternion Heavy Industries is a major cargo airframe manufacturer and competitor to the Hildebrandt Corporation, specializing in multi-slip sliders.
keyFacts: Ternion Heavy Industries is a direct competitor to the Hildebrandt Corporation.
They manufacture airframes designed for transporting cargo.
Their specialty is multi-slip sliders.
Multi-slip sliders can quickly drop into uninhabited worlds for traffic-free transport.

Org/The Scale.md.title: The Scale
Org/The Scale.md.summary: The Scale is an extranational organization serving the Judge of Ages, enforcing order, contracts, and balance across Færrin from their Stronghold in the Opaline Mountains.
keyFacts: Serves as the Judge of Ages' hand in imposing order and balance across Færrin.
Headquartered in a Stronghold citadel in the Opaline Mountains, built on a leyline conjunction.
Operates as an extranational governance body with stronger guarantees than any nation.
Pactbrokers (most common) witness and magically enforce contracts between parties.
Arbiters track down those who have escaped justice and deliver punishment.
Overseers prevent attempts to seal the Gods away again, averting a new Dark Ages.
Timekeepers prevent meddling with the time stream and stop paradox creation.
Contract magic monitors signees; breaking a contract brings the full might of the Scale.

Org/Verdant Holdings.md.title: Verdant Holdings
Org/Verdant Holdings.md.summary: Verdant Holdings is an agricultural organization focused on high-density, high-yield crops to meet growing population demands.
keyFacts: Specializes in high-density, high-yield crop production
Operates as an agricultural powerhouse
Aims to keep pace with a rapidly growing population

Org/index.md.title: Org
Org/index.md.summary: On Færrin, multinational organizations called Orgs fill the power gaps left by governments, born from rapid prosperity enabled by divine blessings.
keyFacts: On Færrin, Orgs handle safety and order instead of governments.
Divine blessings caused prosperity that outpaced existing power structures.
Orgs rose by capitalizing on the resulting gaps in power.
Forster Cain is the Founder of Prime Meridian and Architect of the Org.
Cain grew up poor in Sableclutch, unable to afford school.
As a child, Cain observed urban dysfunction in Hallia from a church rooftop.
Cain sketched imaginary city plans in the dust on the church roof.
His childhood observations inspired his lifelong question: 'What if I could fix this?'

Org/Ætherion Limited.md.title: Ætherion Limited
Org/Ætherion Limited.md.summary: Ætherion Limited is a continent-wide electricity supplier that operates power plants to provide power for electric appliances.
keyFacts: Ætherion Limited operates power plants across the continent.
They are the primary electricity supplier for electric appliances in the setting.

Phenomena/Harmony/Voidsong.md.title: Voidsong
Phenomena/Harmony/Voidsong.md.summary: The Voidsong is a mysterious, haunting melody heard across the world, experienced in dreams and waking life, dividing society between believers, skeptics, and the fearful.
keyFacts: The Voidsong belongs to the Divine and Occult traditions.
It is first experienced as a dream involving twin suns, a riverbank, and an unidentifiable singing figure.
The melody is described as haunting, filled with loneliness and longing — like a funeral dirge or call to action.
Hearers cannot recall the specific notes, yet the melody remains unmistakably present.
The Voidsong has spread publicly, appearing in radio broadcasts, screamsheet editorials, and whispered conversations.
Society is divided over it: some call it revelation, others a trap, others fear it is extraterrestrial.
The Voidsong is said to have 'marked' those who hear it, driving them to seek answers.
The search for answers leads seekers to Hallia and the organization Iconoclasm.

Phenomena/Harmony/index.md.title: Harmony
Phenomena/Harmony/index.md.summary: Harmony is the form of magic on Færrin, ranging from subtle background forces to agentive powers that bond with chosen Listeners.
keyFacts: Some harmonies are soft and subtle, unfelt in daily life.
Other harmonies have agency and will themselves to be known by chosen individuals.
Agentive harmonies connect their Listeners together.
Harmonies can impart knowledge or power on those who accept them.
The origin of harmonies is unknown.

Phenomena/Hearts.md.title: Hearts
Phenomena/Hearts.md.summary: Hearts are beings formed by infusing a divine spark into an idea. They are sentient, have fixed physical forms, and are bound to magical dwellings called fanes.
keyFacts: Hearts are created by combining a divine spark with an idea, resulting in a tangible being.
A heart chooses its physical form once, and cannot change it afterward.
Hearts live in fanes — magical dwellings smaller on the outside, which the heart controls and can reshape.
Hearts cannot leave their fane; attempting to do so causes them to explode.
Fanes vary in size from a mousehole to a city; multiple small fanes can merge, and fanes grow with more magic.
Hearts are sentient with thoughts and feelings, but tend to be single-minded due to their origin from ideas.
Hearts communicate via a magical network called the skein; outsiders can join via a magic item called a totem.
Hearts can project outside their fane briefly when summoned by someone holding their totem.

Phenomena/Infinite Horizon.md.title: Infinite Horizon
Phenomena/Infinite Horizon.md.summary: The Infinite Horizon is a phenomenon making Færrin one of an infinite chain of parallel worlds, connected by ephemeral links traversable with simple magic devices called slides.
keyFacts: Færrin exists as an infinite chain of parallel versions of itself, with connections forming between adjacent worlds.
The two directions of travel are called 'voidward' and 'starward,' with Færrin prime as the origin point.
The most basic method of traversal is a magic device called a 'slide'; some individuals can slip without one.
Adjacent worlds near Færrin prime were found empty — as if the planet had never been inhabited.
The first sentient life discovered was a sprite at Star 46,656, itself having traveled from further starward.
Exploration is currently halted by 'walls': travelers entering Star 1,679,616 or Void 1,953,125 have never returned.
Resource and land scarcity are non-issues since the same mines and fields exist across infinite worlds.
Faster traversal methods include random 'chutes,' powerful teleportation magic, and vehicles called Sliders.

Rules/Listener Dedication.md.title: Listener Dedication
Rules/Listener Dedication.md.summary: A special character dedication for Listeners — people whose souls have been shaped by a Harmony singing across the Horizon.
keyFacts: All characters start with the Listener Dedication in addition to normal creation options.
Listeners are connected to a Harmony, an enigmatic force that binds them to others.
Harmonies can choose Listeners from any creed or walk of life; the selection process is unknown.
Public reactions to Listeners include skepticism, disbelief, reverence, fear, and indifference.
Hearing a Harmony causes attunement, granting the Listener that Harmony's trait.
A character can be attuned to at most one Harmony at a time.
If offered attunement to a second Harmony, the Listener must choose only one to keep.
Choral abilities gain the user's Harmony trait added to them if the user has a Harmony trait.

Rules/Resonance Spells.md.title: Resonance Spells
Rules/Resonance Spells.md.summary: Resonance spells are a special spell type tied to Harmony attunement, cast using a dedicated Resonance pool rather than standard spell slots.
keyFacts: Resonance spells are gained only through special class features or feats, not chosen from a spell list.
They are cast using a Resonance pool, completely separate from normal spell slots.
Resonance spells auto-heighten to half your level rounded up, like cantrips.
Casting a resonance spell costs 1 Resonance; cantrips cost nothing but require a Resonance pool.
Max Resonance pool size equals the number of resonance spells known, or 3, whichever is lower.
Resonance cantrips do not count toward the pool's maximum size.
Resonance replenishes fully on daily preparations; Refocus lets you regain 1 Resonance per use.
Resonance spells share the tradition of their granting Harmony; the Voidsong is both Divine and Occult.

Timeline.md.title: Timeline
Timeline.md.summary: A chronological timeline of major world events in Færrin, from prehistoric mundanity through 863ag when the Party joins Iconoclasm.
keyFacts: Before 0ag: A crack in the firmament floods Færrin with magic; warlords seize power, beginning the Dark Ages.
0ag: The Iridescent Host instructs Hierophant Aurelia to widen the crack, ending the Dark Ages and enabling divine magi…
41ag: Aurelia receives the secret of creating divine life; the first Heart, Rule of Three, is born in the Iridescent Ch…
148ag: Obie Toombs accidentally discovers the Infinite Horizon in Istria by creating the first slide.
212–254ag: Aurthalaxus the Plaguebearer devastates Fūlwinter and the Siksithys Kingdom before being slain by Aurelia II…
515ag: Forster Cain and Aurelia V perform a bloodless coup, deposing Calaria's royal family and installing an Org/Churc…
823ag: Calaria destroys Raelion on the Night of the Midnight Sun, leaving a gaping magic wound in the earth.
862ag: A wall of fire seals Teller's Run in Hallia; it is declared a sovereign city-state via accord between the Church…

index.md.title: Heart of Hearts
index.md.summary: A recovered log from the CIC Rule Of Three, capturing a three-part internal dialogue about receiving forbidden divine knowledge from their creator.
keyFacts: The log is designated signal sequence file №00-000-02, timestamp @ts000.01.01.000.00.018
The CIC Rule Of Three has three distinct voices: Left (L), Center (C), and Right (R)
L speaks in reverent, poetic language about divine light and ascension
R insists the divine knowledge is forbidden and reserved only for a chosen Hierophant
C argues they are the 'selected' — made by their creator specifically for this task
A female creator ('she') opened a path and is referenced as having a mortal vessel
The log describes the knowledge as too vast for a mortal body and too dangerous to write down
All three voices ultimately converge on the word 'glory'
